commands.repair_reason: Contributor-side real behavior proof is still needed, and automation cannot prove the reporter’s Pi/systemd duplicate-service setup for them.; Cleared: The diff only changes gateway CLI startup ordering and a colocated unit test; it does not add dependencies, downloads, CI changes, secret handling, or broader permissions. (sha=7672021a1954883411afbca57ed0620ed0d07782)
commands.review_summary:
The branch moves service-mode gateway stale PID cleanup until after bind resolution and a `/healthz` probe, exits early when a healthy listener already owns the configured port, and adds a focused regression test.

Reproducibility: yes. with high confidence from source inspection and incident logs: current main runs service-mode stale PID cleanup before the later supervised health arbitration, and the PR body shows duplicate systemd services killing each other on port 18789. I did not run the live Pi scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Contributor-side real behavior proof is still needed, and automation cannot prove the reporter’s Pi/systemd duplicate-service setup for them.

target.title: Fix gateway service startup race
target.body:
## Summary
- Fix a gateway service startup race where one service-mode gateway can kill another healthy gateway listener before the normal gateway lock/startup path runs.
- Move service-mode stale listener cleanup behind bind resolution and a `/healthz` probe so duplicate boot entries leave the already healthy gateway in control.
- Add a regression covering the service-mode case where a healthy existing gateway must not call stale PID cleanup or start a second server.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: On the affected Raspberry Pi, two boot-managed OpenClaw gateway entries targeted the same port (`18789`). During startup, each service-mode gateway process treated the other gateway process as stale, killed it before the lock/startup path could arbitrate ownership, and then restarted. This produced a repeated boot/restart loop and made the gateway appear stopped or unstable.

Real environment tested: Raspberry Pi running Linux/systemd. Gateway port `18789`. Old duplicate system unit `/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service` was enabled before cleanup and ran OpenClaw service v2026.5.5 with command shape `gateway --port 18789`. Intended user unit `~/.config/systemd/user/openclaw-gateway.service` was enabled through root linger and ran OpenClaw service v2026.5.12 with command shape `gateway run --port 18789`. `loginctl show-user root -p Linger` returned `Linger=yes`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Checked live gateway processes with `pgrep -af '[o]penclaw.*gateway|[n]ode .*openclaw.*gateway'`.
2. Checked the gateway listener with `ss -ltnp 'sport = :18789'`.
3. Checked the system service with `systemctl status openclaw-gateway.service --no-pager` and `systemctl is-enabled openclaw-gateway.service`.
4. Checked the user service with `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/0 systemctl --user status openclaw-gateway.service --no-pager`.
5. Read the gateway file log at `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-18.log` for the repeated startup/kill sequence.
6. Disabled the duplicate old system unit with `systemctl disable --now openclaw-gateway.service`.
7. Rechecked process count, listener state, user service state, and `openclaw gateway status --deep`.

Evidence after fix: Before cleanup, live process checks showed two gateway processes at the same time, one with command shape `gateway run --port 18789` and one with command shape `gateway --port 18789`.

The live gateway log showed repeated service-mode stale cleanup and termination. Representative redacted runtime log excerpt:

```text
killing 1 stale gateway process(es) before restart: <pid>
service-mode: cleared 1 stale gateway pid(s) before bind on port 18789
signal SIGTERM received
```

The old system service was confirmed as enabled and active before cleanup:

```text
Loaded: loaded (/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service; enabled; preset: enabled)
Active: active (running)
CGroup: /system.slice/openclaw-gateway.service
  node ... openclaw/dist/index.js gateway --port 18789
```

After cleanup, the same machine had one user-service gateway process and one listener:

```text
systemctl is-enabled openclaw-gateway.service
disabled

XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/0 systemctl --user is-enabled openclaw-gateway.service
enabled

XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/0 systemctl --user is-active openclaw-gateway.service
active

pgrep -af '[o]penclaw.*gateway|[n]ode .*openclaw.*gateway'
one process: node ... openclaw/dist/index.js gateway run --port 18789

ss -ltnp 'sport = :18789'
LISTEN 0 511 0.0.0.0:18789 ... users:(("node",pid=<pid>,fd=25))
```

Copied live output from the OpenClaw gateway status probe after cleanup:

```text
Connectivity probe: ok
Capability: admin-capable
Gateway version: 2026.5.12
Listening: *:18789
```

Observed result after fix: The operational issue was caused by a duplicate local service setup, but the code path allowed that duplicate boot entry to become a restart storm by killing a healthy peer before checking health/ownership. With this patch, a service-mode gateway first probes `/healthz`; if a healthy gateway already owns the port, the new process exits and leaves the existing gateway in control instead of running stale PID cleanup.

What was not tested: I did not run broad local gates (`pnpm check`) on the Raspberry Pi because that is too heavy for this host and previously destabilized it. I did not reboot the Pi after the code patch because the duplicate old system unit was already disabled during operational recovery. The focused regression covers the exact startup race this PR changes.

## Verification
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: thiagocaltoe
